title: 告别「盯屏守候」：Claude Code 高级自动化三层框架
author: Claude
content:
Stop Babysitting Your Agents: Advanced Automation Patterns with Claude Code

As AI models grow increasingly capable, software engineers frequently encounter a frustrating paradox: instead of fully outsourcing tasks, they spend a large portion of their workdays staring at their screens, waiting for generations to complete, or acting as glorified QA testers. In this talk, Sid Bidasaria, a founding engineer of Claude Code, walks through the technical paradigm shifts required to break free from this pattern and confidently delegate engineering workloads to autonomous agents.

The Prerequisites: Table Stakes

Before implementing advanced workflows, software teams must configure three fundamental pillars to maximize agent context and capabilities:

  • High-Quality CLAUDE.md File: This configuration document acts as the single highest-leverage intervention to establish codebase context, outline project rules, and guide agent behavior.
  • Comprehensive Tool Integration: AI agents flourish when provided with the identical toolchain humans rely on. Connecting external platforms—such as Slack, Linear, Asana, Datadog, or BigQuery—allows Claude to build rich situational context and manage data-intensive workflows.
  • Cloud-Decoupled Environments: Transitioning terminal execution paths to Claude Code Web (claude.ai/code) isolates the agent compute resource inside remote containers. This shields multi-hour sessions from local laptop disruptions, water spills, or network losses.

Reconsidering Tooling for Autonomous Agents

Traditional development tooling—ranging from syntax linters and type checkers to IDE layouts and compilers—was designed explicitly around the cognitive speeds and limitations of human developers. However, because agents are generating a major share of active codebases, development environments must adapt to accommodate non-human workers.

While primary code parsers translate effectively to autonomous workflows, developers have massive operational blind spots regarding the unique needs of an agent. Optimizing workflows requires teams to evaluate what structural details a software agent requires that a human engineer implicitly assumes or extracts contextually from a directory.

The Three-Tier Architectural Roadmap

  +------------------+     +------------------+     +------------------+
  |   Verification   | --> | Parallelization  | --> | Background Loops |
  |   (Self-Testing) |     |  (Multi-Claude)  |     | (Hands-off Cron) |
  +------------------+     +------------------+     +------------------+

1. Verification Loops

Verification loops introduce autonomous testing circuits that empower Claude to inspect, iterate, and continuously hill-climb toward a predefined success state. Instead of submitting unverified scripts, the agent follows an established human playbook:

  1. Modifies codebase architecture.
  2. Triggers local application compilers or type checkers.
  3. Launches local runtime structures (e.g., web servers or Docker instances).
  4. Inspects operational side effects using automation tools like the Chrome Model Context Protocol (MCP) tool via /chrome or Playwright.
  5. Analyzes application errors or linter feedback within an enclosed feedback loop until reaching an optimal build state.

To make these patterns portable, teams can package verification playbooks inside local Skills. By including precise recursive text directives, these skills become self-documenting and self-improving assets that dynamically adjust their instructions whenever an agent hits an infrastructure blocker.

2. Multi-Clauding & Managing Scale

Running numerous independent AI tasks in parallel quickly drains human attention networks. To mitigate this cognitive load, developers can organize concurrent streams through dedicated interface frameworks:

  • Claude Code Desktop GUI: A centralized control plane featuring a unified sidebar to organize, name, color-code, and pin active sessions spanning disparate git repositories or remote cloud nodes.
  • Claude Agents Terminal View: Accessible via claude agents, this local environment catalogs open active shells and automatically bubble-sorts them by the degree of human intervention they require (e.g., isolating sessions waiting for token prompts or runtime validation permissions at the top).
  • Mobile Remote Control: Activating /remote-control binds long-running workflows to a mobile application. This routes process notifications directly to a smartphone, enabling developers to review permissions or answer agent dependencies on the move.

3. Detached Background Execution

Significant developer overhead centers on maintenance tasks such as monitoring open Pull Requests, responding to reviewer comments, handling merge conflicts, synchronizing API docs, and triaging exception logs. Moving this bookkeeping out of the hot path relies on automated background execution structures:

  • The /loop Command: Instructs a local Claude Code terminal instance to execute targeted instructions (e.g., /loop 10m "babysit my open PRs and resolve conflicts") at distinct time intervals.
  • Remote Routines: Executes cloud-hosted agent tasks decoupled from local environments. Operating inside persistent containers, these workflows respond to specific clock schedules (cron) or explicit platform hooks—enabling the Claude Code team to automatically compile daily documentation additions and broadcast triaged support insights over internal Slack channels every six hours.

By stacking reliable self-verification, organized multi-session orchestration, and cloud-hosted routine intervals together, teams shift their technical focus toward core system designs while delegating low-level debugging and maintenance entirely to AI infrastructure.
aiSummary: Claude Code 创始工程师 Sid Bidasaria 提出验证循环、多智能体并行与后台执行三层框架，帮助开发者彻底摆脱手动「陪跑」智能体的困境，实现全自主工程工作流。
oneSentenceSummary: Claude Code 创始工程师 Sid Bidasaria 提出验证循环、多智能体并行与后台执行三层框架，帮助开发者彻底摆脱手动「陪跑」智能体的困境，实现全自主工程工作流。
summary:
在这场大会演讲中，Claude Code 创始工程师 Sid Bidasaria 直击一个日益突出的悖论：AI 模型越来越强大，开发者却要花更多时间盯着屏幕等待结果。他围绕三层架构路线图，系统讲解如何一步步实现「无人值守」工程协作。

第一层是验证循环：让 Claude 按照人类开发者的测试 SOP 自主校验工作成果——写代码、编译、启动运行时、通过 Chrome MCP 或 Playwright 进行浏览器自动化、检查副作用，并持续迭代直至达到预设的成功状态。Sid 以开源项目 Monkeytype 进行了现场演示：Claude 自动启动开发服务器，用 Chrome MCP 操作 UI，发现 lint 报错后自动修复并验证，全程无需人工介入。这套验证 SOP 还可以被封装进「自进化」的 Skill 文件——每次智能体遇到阻塞就自动更新文档，最终形成全团队共同维护的活文档。

第二层聚焦多智能体并行策略，解决同时管理多个 Claude 会话时的注意力超载问题。工具包包括：跨界面会话管理的 Claude Code Desktop 侧边栏、按紧急程度自动排序的终端视图 `claude agents`、与本地机器解耦的 Claude Code Web，以及把智能体通知推送到手机的 `/remote-control` 命令。

第三层彻底将人从重复性开销中解放出来——PR 巡查、文档更新、CI 监控等任务，通过 `/loop` 命令按时间间隔本地执行，或借助 Remote Routines 在云端按 cron 或事件触发运行。Claude Code 团队自身就在用 Routines：每天自动更新文档，每六小时向 Slack 推送 Issue 摘要。

mainPoints.point: 高质量的 CLAUDE.md 加上完善的工具集成，是一切高级自动化的前提条件。
mainPoints.explanation: Sid 指出三项基础要素：精心打磨的 CLAUDE.md 是回报率最高的单一配置项；接入 Slack、Linear、Datadog、BigQuery 等工具可为 Claude 提供更丰富的上下文；Claude Code Web 则让会话彻底摆脱对本地笔记本的依赖。

mainPoints.point: 验证循环让 Claude 能够模仿人类开发者的测试流程，自主爬坡直至达成成功状态。
mainPoints.explanation: 赋予 Claude 编译、启动开发服务器、通过 Chrome MCP 或 Playwright 驱动浏览器、读取日志等能力后，团队就能构建出闭合反馈回路：Claude 写代码、检测失败、修复问题、重新验证，全流程无需人工 QA，PR 质量显著提升。

mainPoints.point: 自进化的 Skill 文件将验证经验沉淀为团队共享的活文档，持续自我更新。
mainPoints.explanation: Skill 将验证 SOP 存储在可移植的 Markdown 文件中，并内置「每次遇到基础设施阻塞就更新文档」的指令。最终形成一份全团队共同贡献、不断演进的知识资产，大幅减少重复排查的成本。

mainPoints.point: `claude agents` 视图和 Desktop 侧边栏有效缓解并行运行四个以上会话时的认知过载。
mainPoints.explanation: 两个界面均按紧急程度自动排序——等待权限确认的阻塞会话会置顶显示——同时支持重命名、颜色标记和固定操作，帮助开发者在多个并行 worktree 中精准分配注意力。

mainPoints.point: `/loop` 命令和 Remote Routines 将重复性维护任务彻底移出开发者的工作主路径。
mainPoints.explanation: 通过 `/loop 10m` 持续巡查开放的 PR，或将文档日更和 Slack 摘要推送配置为云端 Routine，可以把系统性的运维开销全部交给常驻后台的自动化，让开发者专注于深度工作。

keyQuotes: 随着模型越来越聪明，我发现我们花在盯屏幕上的时间占比反而越来越大——要么等 Claude 完成任务，要么充当 Claude 的「高级 QA 测试员」。
Lint 工具、IDE、Prettier、类型检查器乃至编译器，当初设计它们的出发点都是让人类和人类团队跑得更快。但问题是，现在写代码的主力已经不是人类，而是智能体。
循环本质上是一条你可以为 Claude 闭合的自主回路，让 Claude 能够在给定任务或成功标准上持续爬坡。
Skill 还有一个有趣之处：你可以让它自我进化。只要在 Skill 里写入「每次遇到阻塞就改进 Skill」的指令，你就会得到一份自我记录、自我进化的 Skill，团队里所有人都可以向它贡献，而不只是你一个人。
`/loop` 是在 Claude Code 中以固定间隔运行 prompt 的方式。比如输入 `/loop 10m` 加上「帮我盯着开放的 PR」，就可以让它持续工作了。
